items.hook: The United States and Cuba resume full diplomatic relations after five decades.
items.body: The United States of America (USA), also known as the United States (U.S.) or America, is a country primarily located in North America. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.hook: The Battle of Kellistown was fought on this day between the forces of the English led by Roger Mortimer, 4th Earl of March against the O'Byrnes and O'Tooles under the command of Art Óg mac Murchadha Caomhánach, the most powerful Chieftain in Leinster.

items.hook: Hunter-gatherers introduced fish to a mountain lake 7000 years ago
items.body: Many centuries before the advent of agriculture in Norway, ancient people transported fish into mountain lakes to create a source of food, then built wooden traps to catch them

items.hook: Why a Ukrainian cruise missile is flying with hobby drone hardware
items.body: An open-source flight-control system found in consumer drones has been installed in Ukraine’s latest cruise missile, showing how cheap technology is making military hardware accessible to all

items.hook: Café Rouge is a French-styled restaurant chain, with 6 sites across the United Kingdom, 5 of which are within Center Parcs resorts.
items.body: Café Rouge is part of a bigger restaurant network owned by The Big Table. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: Richard I of England officially invested as Duke of Normandy.
items.body: Richard I, known as Richard the Lionheart or Richard Cœur de Lion because of his reputation as a great military leader and warrior, was King of England from 1189 until his death in 1199. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: Turkish invasion of Cyprus: Forces from Turkey invade Cyprus after a coup d'état, organized by the dictator of Greece, against president Makarios.
items.body: Turkey invaded Cyprus on 20 July 1974 in an operation that progressed in two phases over the following month. One of those things you can't un-know.
